description: The figure presents a linear regression analysis for low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel subjected to isothermal hot compression at 1050 °C and a strain rate of 0.01 s⁻¹. It plots ln(σ/σp) on the vertical axis against [ln(ε/εp) + 1 − ε/εp] on the horizontal axis, where σp and εp denote peak stress and peak strain, respectively. Black cross markers represent discrete experimental data points, while the red solid line shows the linear fit. The regression yields a material constant C = 0.1461 with a correlation coefficient R = 0.9728, demonstrating a strong linear relationship. This fitting procedure, based on the Cingara-McQueen equation, enables extraction of the material constant required to determine the critical strain for dynamic recrystallization (DRX). The result supports the constitutive modeling of DRX kinetics and validates the applicability of this analytical approach for describing the rheological behavior of Fe-Mn-Al-C steel under the specified thermomechanical processing condition.
